page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled, alongside 192 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent data.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng tests and Passmark CPU scores. Cryptographic capabilities are robust, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets, which is reflected in its strong Passmark encryption benchmark. Memory performance is average, with uncached read bandwidth at 109.91 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 57.35 GB/sec.

The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high parallel processing capacity without the memory overhead of memory-optimized tiers. However, the lack of bundled local storage and GPU hardware limits its suitability for heavy storage-bound databases or deep learning training. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores, it provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, alongside strong cryptographic capabilities and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform within average ranges, with uncached read bandwidth at 109.91 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance represents a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is highly effective for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring forty-eight vCPUs and AMD EPYC processing for multi-threaded workloads and software compilation.

Strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Passmark benchmarks
